route1.interpretation: delta_global (+1.771 dex) is much larger than the TNG-SPARC reference offset (+0.215 dex). This is expected: Yang M_200 includes the full dark matter halo out to the virial radius (~200 kpc), while SPARC kinematic M_dyn is measured only to the last rotation-curve point (~20-50 kpc). The aperture gap dominates. The TNG-SPARC offset (+0.215 dex) is a small fraction (12.1%) of the total Yang-kinematic gap, meaning the TNG M_dyn excess at fixed M_bar is NOT simply 'catching up to the full halo mass' but rather reflects a more concentrated inner mass profile. Route 3 (SPARC internal consistency: Vflat vs outer-point) provides a cleaner diagnostic of estimator bias.

verdict: DIAGNOSTIC: SPARC kinematic M_dyn estimator bias is <0.02 dex; the +0.215 dex TNG offset is consistent with inner concentration differences, not estimator failure.
